data.title: Ether outperforming Bitcoin in US ETF market as BTC ETFs lose $285M; Research predicts ETH to take the lead.
data.description: A significant topic of discussion in the US spot ETF market is the comparison between Ether and Bitcoin. According to Swissblock research, there is a prediction that in the long run, Ether will emerge as the more successful option. This analysis indicates a growing interest in Ether over Bitcoin within the ETF sector in the United States.

data.title: Bybit invests $1M in EthicHub, moving 100 tons of LATAM coffee to premium markets. Partnered with BGA to aid financial growth.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, in Dubai, UAE, it was announced that the world's second-largest cryptocurrency exchange, Bybit, has made notable advancements in its collaboration with EthicHub. EthicHub, a project backed by the Blockchain for Good Alliance (BGA), aims to enhance financial services. Bybit, known for its high trading volume, is strengthening its partnership with EthicHub to work towards achieving this goal.

data.title: Zoomlion invests in global localization with community actions beyond business in Philippines as of July 25, 2025.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, in Manila, Philippines, Zoomlion Heavy Industry Science & Technology Co., Ltd. announced its enhanced focus on localization through various impactful measures. The company is dedicating efforts beyond business operations to deepen its engagement with the local community in the Philippines. This initiative is part of Zoomlion's strategy to prioritize localization and strengthen its presence in the region.

data.title: Charter Communications (NASDAQ:CHTR) likely to report Q2 earnings of $9.78/share and revenue of $13.76 billion, up from previous year. Collaboration with Comcast and T-Mobile. Shares fell 4.6% to $380.00. More details on Benzinga.com.
data.description: Charter Communications, Inc. is set to unveil its second-quarter earnings on July 25. Analysts predict the company, headquartered in Stamford, Connecticut, will post earnings of $9.78 per share, up from $8.49 per share a year ago. Revenue for this quarter is anticipated to be around $13.76 billion, compared to $13.69 billion in the same period last year. Charter recently partnered with Comcast to utilize T-Mobile 5G for their wireless business clients. Following this news, Charter Communications' stocks dropped by 4.6%, closing at $380.00 on Thursday. For more details and updates, readers can refer to the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga.
